flashcards.front: What is the main goal of election algorithms?
flashcards.back: To elect a single coordinator process from a group of processes in a distributed system.

flashcards.front: Name the two main types of election algorithms.
What is the message complexity of the Ring Algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n) where n is the number of processes.
The Bully Algorithm, where higher-ID processes 'bully' lower-ID processes.

flashcards.front: What happens if multiple processes initiate elections simultaneously in the Ring Algorithm?
flashcards.back: The algorithm handles multiple initiations by having each election message collect process IDs, and the highest ID eventually wins.
